mainText:
Good morning. Here's your Breeze communications briefing for Friday, March 27.

Starting with Breeze coverage.
Coverage tone is positive this morning. The Points Guy (March 26) ran a favorable piece on Breeze's summer schedule build-up, highlighting the underserved market strategy and calling out fare competitiveness in secondary cities. No negative Breeze mentions in the last 24 hours across monitored outlets.

Now for industry and competitor news.
Delta (March 26) announced capacity cuts on 15 regional routes in Q. 3, the largest single-quarter reduction since 2020. Three of the cut routes overlap with Breeze's current network — worth asking leadership whether to accelerate marketing in those markets.

F.A.A. (March 27) released updated guidance on airport slot allocation that could limit ULCC scheduling flexibility at congested airports. Could affect Breeze's ability to add frequencies at slot-controlled stations — worth flagging to network planning.

Southwest (March 26) reshuffled its communications leadership team following investor day messaging concerns last week. If their comms operation is in transition, it may create an opening for Breeze to lead the narrative in shared markets.

JetBlue (March 27) reported a 12 percent year-over-year decline in ancillary revenue, citing customer pushback on unbundling. Opportunity to reinforce Breeze's bundled value messaging — could be a pitch angle for trade outlets covering the unbundling debate.

United (March 26) confirmed a new direct route between Salt Lake City and London Heathrow starting fall 2026. Not a direct competitive overlap, but could generate inbound media questions about international ambitions at SLC — worth having a holding statement ready.

Alaska Airlines (March 27) announced a codeshare expansion with Qantas covering six new transpacific routes. No direct Breeze impact, but signals continued consolidation of alliance partnerships among legacy carriers — context for any future partnership conversations.

That's your briefing. Roger is standing by when you're ready to take action on any of these stories.
